general.title: General Prevention Tips for All Crops

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: Rotate crops every 2-3 years to prevent soil-borne disease buildup
tips.implementation: Plant different crop families in each season

tips.title: Use Disease-Free Seeds
tips.description: Always use certified, disease-free seeds from reliable sources
tips.implementation: Purchase from certified suppliers, inspect seeds before planting

tips.title: Proper Spacing
tips.description: Maintain adequate spacing between plants for air circulation
tips.implementation: Follow recommended spacing for each crop variety

tips.title: Sanitize Tools
tips.description: Clean and disinfect gardening tools between uses
tips.implementation: Use bleach solution (1:9) or alcohol to sterilize tools

tips.title: Remove Plant Debris
tips.description: Clear fallen leaves and plant debris regularly
tips.implementation: Compost healthy debris, burn or discard infected material

tips.title: Water Management
tips.description: Water at base of plants, avoid overhead irrigation
tips.implementation: Use drip irrigation or water early morning

tips.title: Mulching
tips.description: Apply organic mulch to prevent soil splash
tips.implementation: Use straw, wood chips, or plastic mulch

tips.title: Regular Monitoring
tips.description: Inspect plants weekly for early disease signs
tips.implementation: Check undersides of leaves, look for spots or discoloration

tips.title: Proper Nutrition
tips.description: Maintain balanced fertilization for plant health

tips.title: Companion Planting
tips.description: Plant companion crops that repel pests
tips.implementation: Marigolds, basil, and nasturtiums are good companions

tips.title: Plant Resistant Varieties
tips.description: Choose apple varieties resistant to common diseases like scab and rust

tips.title: Prune Annually
tips.description: Prune trees during dormancy to improve air circulation

tips.title: Remove Mummified Fruit
tips.description: Remove and destroy mummified fruit that harbor diseases

tips.title: Apply Dormant Oil
tips.description: Apply dormant oil in late winter to control overwintering pests

tips.title: Thin Fruit
tips.description: Thin fruit to improve air circulation and reduce disease pressure

tips.title: Clean Fallen Leaves
tips.description: Rake and remove fallen leaves in autumn to reduce overwintering pathogens

tips.title: Monitor Weather
tips.description: Track weather conditions for infection periods

tips.title: Maintain Tree Health
tips.description: Provide adequate water and nutrients for tree vigor
tips.schedule: Fertilize in spring, water during drought

tips.title: Remove Cedar Trees
tips.description: Remove nearby cedar/juniper trees to prevent rust diseases

tips.title: Plant Resistant Varieties
tips.description: Choose disease-resistant varieties (look for VFN designation)

tips.title: Stake or Cage Plants
tips.description: Support plants to keep foliage off the ground

tips.title: Remove Lower Leaves
tips.description: Prune lower leaves to prevent soil splash

tips.title: Water at Base
tips.description: Avoid wetting foliage when watering
tips.method: Use drip irrigation or soaker hoses

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: Don't plant tomatoes in same spot for 3-4 years

tips.title: Mulch
tips.description: Apply mulch to prevent soil splash

tips.title: Apply Preventive Sprays
tips.description: Apply copper or sulfur sprays preventatively

tips.title: Morning Watering
tips.description: Water in morning so leaves dry quickly

tips.title: Remove Infected Plants
tips.description: Remove and destroy diseased plants immediately
tips.method: Bag and discard, do not compost

tips.title: Clean Up Season End
tips.description: Remove all plant debris at end of season

tips.title: Use Certified Seed Potatoes
tips.description: Always plant certified disease-free seed potatoes
tips.note: Never use grocery store potatoes for planting

tips.title: Hill Soil Around Plants
tips.description: Hill soil around stems to protect tubers

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: Rotate with non-nightshade crops
tips.rotation: 3-4 year rotation with corn, beans, or grains

tips.title: Avoid Overhead Irrigation
tips.description: Keep foliage dry to prevent blight
tips.method: Use drip irrigation or water at base

tips.title: Destroy Volunteer Potatoes
tips.description: Remove potato volunteers that can harbor diseases

tips.title: Harvest Promptly
tips.description: Harvest when mature, don't leave in ground

tips.title: Proper Storage
tips.description: Store tubers in cool, dark, dry place

tips.title: Apply Preventive Fungicides
tips.description: Apply fungicides before disease appears

tips.title: Choose Resistant Varieties
tips.description: Plant disease-resistant grape varieties

tips.title: Proper Pruning
tips.description: Prune annually for air circulation

tips.title: Canopy Management
tips.description: Train vines to allow sunlight and air movement

tips.title: Remove Mummified Berries
tips.description: Remove and destroy mummified fruit

tips.title: Apply Sulfur
tips.description: Apply sulfur for powdery mildew control
tips.caution: Do not apply in hot weather (>90°F)

tips.title: Sanitize Pruning Tools
tips.description: Disinfect tools between vines

tips.title: Monitor Weather
tips.description: Track conditions for disease forecasting

tips.title: Good Air Drainage
tips.description: Plant on slopes for air drainage

tips.title: Plant Resistant Hybrids
tips.description: Choose hybrids with disease resistance
tips.note: Look for resistance to common rust and northern leaf blight

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: Rotate with non-grass crops

tips.title: Tillage
tips.description: Bury crop residue to reduce inoculum

tips.title: Proper Spacing
tips.description: Avoid overcrowding for air circulation

tips.title: Early Planting
tips.description: Plant early to avoid peak disease periods

tips.title: Remove Volunteer Corn
tips.description: Eliminate volunteer corn plants

tips.title: Balanced Fertilization
tips.description: Avoid excess nitrogen which increases susceptibility

tips.title: Disease-Free Seeds
tips.description: Use certified disease-free seeds

tips.title: Avoid Overhead Water
tips.description: Water at base, keep foliage dry

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: 3-4 year rotation away from peppers

tips.title: Proper Spacing
tips.description: Allow air circulation between plants

tips.title: Apply Copper Sprays
tips.description: Preventive copper sprays for bacterial diseases

tips.title: Remove Infected Leaves
tips.description: Promptly remove and destroy infected leaves

tips.title: Sanitize Tools
tips.description: Disinfect tools between plants

tips.title: Disease-Free Plants
tips.description: Purchase certified disease-free plants
tips.note: Avoid using plants from unknown sources

tips.title: Proper Spacing
tips.description: Allow air circulation between plants

tips.title: Mulch
tips.description: Apply straw mulch to keep fruit clean

tips.title: Remove Old Leaves
tips.description: Remove old leaves after harvest

tips.title: Avoid Overhead Water
tips.description: Water at base, keep leaves dry

tips.title: Renovate Beds
tips.description: Renovate strawberry beds annually

tips.title: Crop Rotation
tips.description: Plant in new location every 3-4 years
tips.rotation: Avoid areas previously in tomatoes, peppers

spring.name: Spring
tips: Start with disease-free seeds and transplants
Apply preventive fungicides before rain events
Ensure proper spacing for air circulation
Begin regular monitoring for early disease signs
Remove any overwintering plant debris

summer.name: Summer
tips: Water early morning to allow foliage drying
Increase monitoring frequency during humid weather
Apply fungicides preventatively during wet periods
Remove infected leaves immediately
Maintain mulch to prevent soil splash

monsoon.name: Monsoon/Rainy Season
tips: Apply fungicides BEFORE rain events
Ensure proper drainage in fields
Increase air circulation with pruning
Monitor twice daily for disease signs
Avoid working with wet plants
Use copper-based sprays for fungal prevention

winter.name: Winter
tips: Remove all plant debris from fields
Apply dormant oils to fruit trees
Plan crop rotation for next season
Sanitize and store garden tools
Order disease-resistant varieties for spring

conditions.monitoring: Check for disease symptoms next 5-7 days

conditions.action: Protect plants with covers or shade
